methods.description: Process for preparing sediment samples for micropalaeontological study, involving chemical treatment and physical preparation to isolate microfossils.

steps.description: A quantity of approximately 40g of sample to be studied is taken (and crushed if necessary in a mortar).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment sample by weighing and crushing

steps.description: This sample is then poured into a plastic beaker which is marked with the number of the sampled cuttings.
steps.searchable_summary: label and store sample in beaker

steps.description: Each sample is then attacked with hydrogen peroxide, i.e. 10% hydrogen peroxide.
The mixture (hydrogen peroxide + sediment) is stirred with a rod until the reaction is complete.
The product is left to stand for an average of 12 hours.
There follows a series of preparations for the study which consists of washing, drying, bagging the samples and sorting the microfossils.
steps.searchable_summary: wash, dry, bag, and sort microfossils

methods.description: Standard laboratory technique for preparing samples for palynological analysis, involving chemical treatment to isolate organic material.

steps.description: Each sample of excavated material will undergo a palynological preparation which consists in destroying all the mineral phases to keep only the organic phase generally constituted of sporopollinic or palynomorphic material.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sample by removing mineral phases

steps.description: The technique used is the standard technique of palynology laboratories using strong acids (HCl and HF) to extract the palynomorphs.
steps.searchable_summary: use HCl and HF to extract palynomorphs

steps.description: The laboratory preparation for palynological analysis begins with sampling, which consists of taking 20g of sample and putting it in a 2-liters beaker numbered according to a particular dimension, thus avoiding errors.
steps.searchable_summary: sample 20g and store in numbered beaker

steps.description: Then comes the chemical treatment phase which aims to eliminate all the mineral matter contained in the sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: eliminate mineral matter with chemical treatment
